title: Pi RL flywheel — 4 new extensions porting self-driving features from main
summary: Merged origin/main into hathbanger/pi (clean, no conflicts), then built 4 new Pi extensions that port the Claude-specific self-driving loop, policy head, training buffer, and eval system to native Pi tools and commands.
detail:
1. MERGE: Pulled origin/main into hathbanger/pi — brought in policy head (policy-head.ts), training buffer (training-buffer.ts), tuple miner (tuple-miner.ts), autoresearch loop (peter.ts additions), eval pipeline, VM fleet scripts, telemetry agent, CI setup. Clean merge, zero conflicts.

2. policy-head-tool.ts (276 lines): jfl_policy_score tool — agent can score candidate actions before executing (predicted reward delta). jfl_policy_rank tool — rank 2-5 actions by predicted reward. /policy command shows weights stats + training buffer stats. Uses dynamic import to resolve PolicyHeadInference from jfl-cli at runtime.

3. training-buffer-tool.ts (368 lines): jfl_training_buffer tool — manual (state, action, reward) tuple capture. jfl_mine_tuples tool — mine tuples from journals, flows, sessions, evals, telemetry. onTrainingAgentEnd() — automatic tuple capture on every agent_end with 2+ turns. /tuples and /mine commands for interactive use.

4. autoresearch.ts (427 lines): /autoresearch [rounds] command — self-driving experiment loop. Each round: git branch → propose experiment (policy head ranking or fallback heuristic) → execute → eval (jest) → keep or revert. Best round gets pushed + PR via gh CLI. Writes journal entries + training tuples per round. Emits MAP events for each round start/end + completion.

5. eval-tool.ts (224 lines): jfl_eval_status tool — latest scores, dimension breakdown, 10-run trends. jfl_eval_compare tool — diff two eval snapshots. /eval command for quick status.

6. index.ts wiring: All 4 extensions registered in session_start. onTrainingAgentEnd wired into agent_end handler (after stratus resolve + eval capture, before HUD update).
incomplete: Autoresearch currently runs tests locally via jest — needs to support configurable eval commands
Policy head requires STRATUS_API_KEY for embeddings — should fall back gracefully
Training buffer auto-capture on agent_end uses heuristic deltas (0.01/-0.005) — needs real eval integration
next: Test with actual Pi installation. Consider adding /experiment command (single experiment vs N rounds). Wire eval-tool to read from both .jfl/eval/eval.jsonl and service-events.jsonl.
learned: Dynamic imports with @ts-ignore pattern works well for optional jfl-cli dependencies from Pi extensions
Pi's agent_end event has messages[] array instead of turnCount — use messages.length
Pi's tool_execution_end uses toolName instead of tool — need to check both fields
The PiContext shim pattern lets us write all sub-extensions against our own interface while the factory in index.ts adapts to the real Pi API

title: Monorepo service onboarding — detection, agents, Pi team generation
summary: Built full monorepo onboarding pipeline: detectMonorepo() finds TS/JS monorepo structure, resolves workspace packages, classifies roles, builds dependency graph. onboardMonorepo() generates per-package agents (apps AND libraries), coordinator agent, Pi team YAML, and DEPENDENCY_GRAPH.md.
detail:
Three new files:

1. monorepo-detector.ts (441 lines): detectMonorepo() checks for turbo.json, nx.json, pnpm-workspace.yaml, package.json workspaces, lerna.json. Resolves workspace globs to actual packages. For each: reads package.json, runs extractServiceMetadata(), classifies as app/package/config/tool, resolves internal dependencies. Builds impact matrix (change X → rebuild Y,Z). Generates monorepo-aware commands (turbo filter, pnpm --filter, nx run). generateDependencyGraphDoc() produces full markdown doc. generateMonorepoAgentName() creates @{root}-{pkg} names.

2. monorepo-onboard.ts (497 lines): onboardMonorepo() orchestrates the full flow. writeCoordinatorAgent() generates @{name}-coordinator with full dep graph awareness, routing rules, cross-cutting workflow. writePackageAgent() generates per-package agents with monorepo context (internal deps, consumers, filtered commands). generatePiTeam() writes YAML team definition with coordinator + all packages. Updates .jfl/config.json (registered_services with monorepo structure) and projects.manifest.json.

3. init.ts changes (65 lines): After resolving service path, calls detectMonorepo(). If detected: shows package summary with apps/libs, shows agent plan, asks confirmation, calls onboardMonorepo(). Falls through to single-service flow if user declines.

Tested against 5-package Turborepo fixture (@my-platform/web, @my-platform/api, @my-platform/shared-types, @my-platform/ui-kit, @my-platform/config). Correct detection, dependency graph, impact matrix, 6 agent files, Pi team YAML, DEPENDENCY_GRAPH.md.
incomplete: Only TS/JS monorepos (no Go, Rust, Python monorepo detection yet)
No jfl services add command integration (only init flow)
Package descriptions could be richer — pull from README if available
No workspace-level .env detection for shared env vars
next: Add jfl services add monorepo support (not just init). Consider monorepo health checks. Test against real-world monorepos (Next.js examples, t3-stack, etc.).
learned: Turborepo uses root package.json workspaces field — turbo.json alone isn't enough
pnpm-workspace.yaml uses a YAML packages list, not package.json workspaces
Impact matrix needs transitive closure — changing shared-types affects ui-kit which affects web
Agent naming with @{root}-{pkg} avoids collisions across multiple monorepos in same GTM

title: Pi RL flywheel + monorepo onboarding — full session
summary: Merged origin/main, built 4 Pi RL extensions (policy head, training buffer, autoresearch, eval tools), then built complete monorepo service onboarding with detection, agent generation, and Pi team output.
detail:
SESSION WORK:

1. MERGE: Pulled origin/main into hathbanger/pi — clean merge, zero conflicts. Brought in policy head, training buffer, tuple miner, autoresearch loop, self-driving loop, eval pipeline, VM fleet, telemetry agent, CI setup (~7,700 lines).

2. PI RL FLYWHEEL (1,326 lines, 4 new extensions):
- policy-head-tool.ts: jfl_policy_score + jfl_policy_rank tools for scoring candidate actions
- training-buffer-tool.ts: jfl_training_buffer + jfl_mine_tuples tools, auto-capture on agent_end
- autoresearch.ts: /autoresearch command for self-driving experiment loops
- eval-tool.ts: jfl_eval_status + jfl_eval_compare tools + /eval command
All wired into index.ts session_start + agent_end handlers.

3. MONOREPO ONBOARDING (1,002 lines, 2 new files + init.ts changes):
- monorepo-detector.ts: Detects Turborepo/Nx/pnpm/yarn/npm workspaces/Lerna, resolves packages, classifies roles, builds dependency graph + impact matrix
- monorepo-onboard.ts: Generates coordinator agent, per-package agents (apps AND libs), Pi team YAML, DEPENDENCY_GRAPH.md, updates GTM config
- init.ts: Monorepo detection in service onboarding flow with user confirmation
Tested against 5-package Turborepo fixture — all correct.

4. BUILD + LINK: npm run build + npm link — jfl v0.4.4 globally available.
incomplete: Autoresearch uses jest for eval — needs configurable eval command
No jfl services add monorepo support (only init flow)
next: Test Pi extensions with real Pi installation. Add monorepo support to jfl services add. Test against real-world monorepos. Consider /experiment command (single experiment vs N rounds).
learned: Dynamic imports with @ts-ignore work well for optional jfl-cli deps from Pi extensions
Pi agent_end has messages[] not turnCount — use .length
Turborepo uses root package.json workspaces — turbo.json alone isn't enough
Impact matrix needs transitive closure for correct rebuild paths
Agent naming @{root}-{pkg} avoids collisions across multiple monorepos
